summary.intent: Implement a functional layered tool policy system for the moltis project, fulfilling a GitHub discussion request (#641) for per-channel and per-sender tool gating in mixed-trust environments.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented all 6 policy layers (global, per-provider, per-agent-preset, per-channel-group, per-sender, sandbox), wired them into the runtime, propagated sender_id through all channel handlers, added config template examples, a full docs page, and sandbox policy support across 12+ files in 8 crates with 709 tests passing.

repo: resolve_effective_policy existed in crates/tools/src/policy.rs but was dead code — only called by its own unit tests, never by the runtime which used a separate effective_tool_policy() function
AgentPreset already had tools: PresetToolPolicy, so layer 3 (per-agent-preset) required no schema addition
PromptHostRuntimeContext already carried channel_type, channel_account_id, channel_chat_id, and channel_chat_type — making layer 4 (group policy by chat type) feasible without new plumbing
All 6 channel handlers (Telegram, Discord, Slack, MS Teams, WhatsApp, Matrix) had peer_id available locally but were not including it in ChannelMessageMeta
moltis-config was already a dependency of moltis-tools, so no Cargo.toml changes were needed for the policy refactor
The llama-cpp/CUDA crates fail to build on macOS due to missing toolkit — pre-existing issue unrelated to changes; clippy must be scoped to exclude those crates
channel_binding_from_runtime_context used sender_id: None hardcoded; the fix was to add channel_sender_id to PromptHostRuntimeContext and populate it from params in apply_request_runtime_context

code.finding: resolve_effective_policy was only called by its own unit tests; the runtime used effective_tool_policy() at crates/chat/src/lib.rs:1683 instead
effective_tool_policy() read only global config (config.tools.policy.allow/deny + optional profile), ignoring all per-provider/agent/channel/sender layers
apply_runtime_tool_filters has 4 call sites: list_tools (schema listing), raw_prompt (non-streaming send), full_context (streaming send), and run_with_tools
PromptHostRuntimeContext is the right place to add channel_sender_id so it flows naturally to all downstream consumers including build_tool_context and hook payloads

ChannelsConfig stored channel accounts as HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> with no typed policy substructure; added ChannelToolPolicyOverride and GroupToolPolicy types plus a tool_policy_for_account() helper

workflow: Background agents were used effectively for parallel independent work (docs page generation + sandbox policy implementation) while main thread handled the hook sender_id fix
Clippy must be run with explicit crate scoping on macOS to avoid pre-existing CUDA build failures: cargo clippy -p crate1 -p crate2
cargo fmt --all followed by cargo fmt --all --check is the pattern used for formatting verification
beads issues were created first with dependency chains established before implementation began, then closed as tasks completed

friction: CUDA/llama-cpp crates fail to build on macOS, blocking cargo clippy --workspace and requiring manual crate scoping
apply_runtime_tool_filters had 4 call sites scattered across different functions with different available variables, requiring careful per-site context analysis before updating
channel_binding_from_runtime_context had no access to params, requiring adding a new field to PromptHostRuntimeContext rather than a simpler parameter threading approach
The sandbox agent modified schema/template/validate/policy files in parallel which required review before committing to avoid conflicts with main thread changes
open_items: Dedicated docs/src/tool-policy.md page was created but a link from the main configuration reference page may still be needed
sender_id in channel_binding_from_runtime_context for hook payloads depends on #640 (channel metadata in hook payloads) for full end-to-end coverage
SandboxConfig.tools_policy was added but sandbox layer (layer 6) enforcement logic may need further integration testing in sandboxed deployments
The config template examples are commented-out — consider whether a live integration test should validate the new schema fields parse correctly end-to-end
Respond to GitHub discussion #641 with a summary of the implementation and the new config syntax
